title: Music Lab Creative Workbench
context: The Music Lab UI is an agent transparency window -- the agent does the creative work, you watch and steer. We need to nail down the workflow before any design work.

questions.text: How does a new song start? What triggers the creative process?

options.label: Horus remembers lore and proposes a song
options.description: Agent recalls from /memory, finds a story thread, and says 'I want to write about X'

options.label: Graham gives a prompt or theme
options.description: You say 'write something about whisperheads' or 'something dark in D minor'

options.label: Music discovery triggers it
options.description: /consume-music finds a reference track that sparks an idea, agent proposes building on it

options.label: All of the above, depends on mood
options.description: Any of these can kick it off -- the UI handles all entry points

questions.text: You said the flow starts with /memory recall and /create-story for lyrics. How much story context do you want to see before lyrics appear?

options.label: Full story brief (lore + emotional arc + references)
options.description: Agent shows the complete creative brief: what memory triggered this, the emotional journey, reference tracks

options.label: Just the lyrics with a short rationale
options.description: Skip the story -- show the lyrics and a 2-sentence 'why I wrote this'

options.label: Collapsible story, lyrics prominent
options.description: Lyrics are the main thing, but the story/lore brief is expandable for when you want to dig in

questions.text: When the agent is working (generating, iterating, analyzing), how do you want to steer it?

options.label: Chat panel -- type course corrections
options.description: A chat well where you can say 'darker vocals' or 'the bass is too busy' while the agent works

options.label: Approve/reject gates at key milestones
options.description: Agent pauses at lyrics-done, music-generated, voice-applied and waits for thumbs up/down

options.label: Both -- chat for nudges, gates for major decisions
options.description: Chat is always available, but the agent also stops at critical points for explicit approval

options.label: Mostly autonomous -- just show me the final result
options.description: Let the agent run the full pipeline and present the finished track. Review at the end.

questions.text: How do you give feedback on generated audio? What does the playback experience look like?

options.label: Inline waveform player with A/B comparison
options.description: Play current vs previous round side by side, scrub to specific parts, annotate timestamps

options.label: Simple play/pause with text feedback
options.description: Listen, then type 'the chorus needs more energy' in the chat

options.label: MIR analysis overlay on the waveform
options.description: Show BPM, key, energy, spectral features -- the agent's analysis visible on the audio

options.label: Per-stem playback with solo/mute
options.description: Hear individual stems (vocals, bass, drums, keys) and mute/solo to identify issues

questions.text: What should the transparency window show while the agent works? How deep do you want to see?

options.label: High-level pipeline stages (memory -> story -> lyrics -> music -> voice)
options.description: Just which stage we're in, with a progress indicator and the current output

options.label: Skill chain execution trace
options.description: Show every /skill call, its input/output, timing. Like watching a terminal but prettier.

options.label: Agent reasoning + decisions
options.description: Why the agent chose this reference track, why it picked this voice, what /taxonomy tags drove the emotion

options.label: All of the above, layered
options.description: Pipeline overview at top, expandable into skill trace, expandable into reasoning. Progressive disclosure.

questions.text: The agent uses /consume-music and /discover-music to find reference tracks. How should these appear in the UI?

options.label: Side panel with YouTube embeds
options.description: Playable reference tracks in a sidebar, tagged with why they're relevant (heart taxonomy match)

options.label: Inline cards in the pipeline view
options.description: When the agent finds a reference, it appears as a card in the timeline: 'Found this because sadness+trust'

options.label: Just links in the chat
options.description: Agent says 'I found this track that matches the mood' with a link

questions.text: For /learn-voice and /learn-artist -- how do you want to pick and preview voices?

options.label: Voice gallery with audio samples
options.description: Grid of trained RVC models, each with a 5-second sample. Click to preview, select for the song.

options.label: Agent recommends, you approve
options.description: Agent picks the voice based on /taxonomy heart tags and shows why. You approve or pick different.

options.label: A/B voice comparison on the same lyrics
options.description: Agent renders the same line in 2-3 voices, you pick which fits the song's emotion

questions.text: The convergence loop iterates (generate -> analyze -> fix -> repeat). How should rounds be shown?

options.label: Timeline/carousel of rounds
options.description: Scroll through R1, R2, R3... with diff highlights showing what changed

options.label: Just the latest + a delta summary
options.description: Show current output with a badge: 'R3 -- fixed BPM drift, improved vocal clarity'

options.label: Convergence chart (score over rounds)
options.description: Graph showing quality scores improving across rounds, with the current score highlighted

questions.text: What does 'done' mean for a song? When does the agent stop iterating?

options.label: You say it's done (explicit approval)
options.description: Agent presents final output, you click 'Ship it' or 'Keep going'

options.label: Convergence threshold met
options.description: MIR analysis hits target quality scores (BPM accuracy, key consistency, vocal clarity). Auto-done.

options.label: Both -- converge to threshold, then get human sign-off
options.description: Agent iterates until metrics pass, then presents for your final listen and approval

options.label: Never done -- songs live in the library and can be revisited
options.description: Everything is a draft. You can reopen any song and say 'make the bridge darker'

questions.text: What's your mental model for the overall layout? Think about what you'd see when you open Music Lab.

options.label: Single-pane focus: one song, full screen
options.description: Immersive view of the current song's pipeline. Like a recording studio -- one session at a time.

options.label: Library + detail split
options.description: Left: song library (all projects). Right: selected song's pipeline view. Like an email client.

options.label: Dashboard overview with drill-down
options.description: Grid of active songs with status badges. Click one to enter its pipeline view.

options.label: Terminal-inspired with panels
options.description: Agent output streaming in the main pane, control panels on sides. Like watching tmux with better UX.
